steps.title: Recognize what the sentence describes
steps.content: You place your hands (multiple) between two antennas (multiple). Since this is a straightforward action with no possession involved, the nouns must be plain plurals. Apostrophes mark possession or contractions, not plurality.

steps.title: Apply the apostrophe rule
steps.content:
Apostrophes mark possession or contractions — never simple plurals. "Hands" means more than one hand (no possession), and "antennas" means more than one antenna (no possession).

A — "hand's between the two antenna's": "hand's" = belonging to a hand; "antenna's" = belonging to an antenna. Neither word is possessive in context. Fails.

B — "hands between the two antennas": Plain plurals, no apostrophes. You place multiple hands between multiple antennas. Correct.

C — "hands' between the two antennas'": "hands'" = belonging to the hands; "antennas'" = belonging to the antennas. No possession intended. Fails.

D — "hands' between the two antennas": "hands'" still incorrectly marks the plural "hands" as possessive. Fails.
